summary: Duplicate of #3525 — identical report of SkiaSharp rendering performance being dramatically slower than native Skia C++ on macOS (sub-10 fps vs 120 fps native), filed by the same author with the same benchmark repos.

bugSignals.errorMessage: Sub-10 fps default rendering vs 120 fps native C++; ~40 fps with optimized OpenGL flags

stepsToReproduce: Run the C++ native Skia benchmark — observes ~120 fps
Run the SkiaSharp managed equivalent — observes <10 fps default
With OpenGL flag optimizations, achieves ~40 fps

repoLinks.description: Original benchmark repo comparing native Skia vs SkiaSharp
Fork with downgraded Skia matching SkiaSharp version

versionAnalysis.relevanceReason: The SKGLView pixel format attributes and rendering loop have not changed since 3.116.0.

analysis.summary: This issue is a duplicate of #3525, filed by the same author (mattleibow) with an identical title, description, benchmark repos, and version. The only difference is that #3525 additionally references investigation PR #3418. The underlying bug — SKGLView's default OpenGL configuration using 4x MSAA, VSync, legacy GL profile, and redundant flush calls causing severe performance degradation — is already tracked in #3525.
analysis.rationale: Identical title, body content, benchmark repos, reporter, version, and platform to #3525. The issue body text is nearly verbatim, with #3525 containing one extra PR link. Same author filed both. This is a clear duplicate.

keySignals.text: There is a large performance gap between SkiaSharp and equivalent native Skia C++ code

codeInvestigation.finding: Default pixel format attributes request 4x MSAA (Multisample + SampleBuffers=1 + Samples=4), DoubleBuffer, 24-bit depth, and 32-bit color. These are unnecessary overhead for 2D rendering and match the bottleneck described in both #3552 and #3525.

DrawRect() includes backing scale factor tracking, renderTarget validity check, SKAutoCanvasRestore, and triple flush (canvas.Flush + context.Flush + OpenGLContext.FlushBuffer). This redundant flushing and per-frame overhead matches the performance analysis in #3525's triage.

GRContext.Flush() calls gr_direct_context_flush_and_submit. SKCanvas.Flush() also delegates to GRContext.Flush(), so the DrawRect double-flush pattern calls the GPU pipeline twice per frame — confirmed in both issues.

actionability.reason: Identical issue to #3525 — same reporter, same title, same body, same benchmark repos, same version, same platform. #3525 is the original with additional context (PR #3418 reference).

actions.description: Apply bug, views, macOS, OpenGL, performance labels
